name: Brunswick Golf Club
about: The Brunswick Golf Club is an 18-hole championship course located in midcoast Maine, with origins dating back to the late 1800s. The club offers four sets of tees for all skill levels and is known for being walkable, with tree-lined fairways and well-maintained greens. It features a full dining and banquet facility, making it suitable for events of various sizes. The venue also operates a Virtual Golf Club of Brunswick for year-round play and utilizes state-of-the-art technology like TRACKMAN for lessons and club fittings. The club emphasizes reasonable pricing and welcomes both members and the public, positioning itself as a 'Must Play' course in Maine.
why_golfers_like_it: Historic 18-hole course with a rich legacy dating to the late 1800s
Four sets of tees available for all skill levels
State-of-the-art TRACKMAN launch monitor for advanced swing analysis and club fitting

food_and_drink.notes: Full dining facility available for events and casual dining
Full bar service available for members and guests

simulator_hardware.notes: PGA Tour-proven launch monitor used for swing analysis and club fitting

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you will need to bring your own clubs. The club also offers club rentals and professional club fitting services using TRACKMAN technology.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Outside food and drinks are not permitted. The club has a full dining and bar facility available for members and guests.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The time for 18 holes varies depending on pace of play, but the club is known for being walkable and accommodates various tee times throughout the day.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the club offers junior golf camps for ages 7-14 and provides programs for young golfers to learn and develop their skills.
